name: Sun Dried Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 3 lbs. firm ripe Italian or plum tomatoes (Roma)
1/2 teaspoon herb seasoning, oregano, basil, or any herb combination) per tomato
2 to 3 cloves garlic chopped finely, or minced
2 rosemary sprigs or 1 T. dried herbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300 degrees.
2. Choose tomatoes with an even red color and without any green or black spots.
3. Wash tomatoes, removing stem ends. Slice lengthwise in half. Gently squeeze to remove seeds. Pat dry.
4. Sprinkle herbs, garlic, and salt over the tomatoes.
5. Arrange tomatoes in a single layer, cut side up, on wire racks placed on a cookie sheet.
6. Bake for 3-5 hours or until dried, watching carefully not to overdry as they will become tough. They are ready when all moisture is gone; watch for darkening and burning quickly.
7. Transfer tomatoes to a jar with rosemary sprigs and peppercorns. Cover with olive oil and seal tightly.
8. Loosely pack the jar and let stand for 1 1/2 months to develop flavor.
9. Store in a cool place. Tomatoes will stay fresh as long as the oil stays fresh.
